text.title: The twelve phases
text.summary: The orchestrator runs a fixed sequence of phases, each fronted by exactly one prompt file. Phases may be skipped if a playbook says so, but their order is invariant.

list.item: 1. INTAKE — analyzers read each feature folder under `.aidocs/<project>/todo/<feature-id>/` and produce a structured report with open questions.
2. CLARIFY — open questions are deduplicated and asked of the user via batched `AskUserQuestion` calls in Ukrainian.

3. PLAN — one planning subagent per feature writes/updates the SDD artifact set: `spec.md`, `plan.md` (one file with `## Design` + `## Execution Plan` H2 sections per plan-md-structure), conditional `user-flows.md` (user-facing) and `ui-ux-design.md` (UI), atomic `TASK_*.md`.
4. PARALLELIZE — the orchestrator inspects file overlap and groups features into execution waves.
5. BASELINE — for visual fixes, capture a focused before-screenshot of the bug area with a red CSS outline.
6. EXECUTE — wave-scoped subagents apply the fix in isolated worktrees and merge into the local default branch (no remote push).
7. RESOLVE — when waves conflict, a dedicated resolver subagent reads both sides' SDD intent and combines them.
8. VERIFY — build, type-check, lint, unit tests, e2e tests, and `validate-methodology-xml.py` run against the merged result.
9. REVIEW — an independent reviewer reads each feature's diff against its `spec.md` / `plan.md` (and `user-flows.md` / `ui-ux-design.md` if applicable) and returns PASS / FAIL-WITH-NITS / FAIL.
10. FIX — review blockers are addressed by a fix-applier subagent, then VERIFY and REVIEW are repeated (hard cap on iterations).
11. RECAPTURE — focused after-screenshot with a green outline; before+after pair delivered out-of-band with explanatory captions.
12. DELIVER — feature folder moved `in-progress/ → done/`, commits pushed and artifacts deployed only after explicit user confirmation.

section.title: Prompt file map
section.p: Each phase has exactly one prompt file. Naming uses a numeric prefix so the directory listing reads as the phase order. Prompt files live next to the orchestrator's runtime root (typically `.aidocs/orchestrator/prompts/` or a per-batch `prompts/` folder); this methodology only ships the skeleton — see `templates/prompt-skeleton.md`.

list.item: `prompts/01-intake-analyzer.md` — INTAKE
`prompts/02-clarification-collector.md` — CLARIFY
`prompts/03-sdd-planner.md` — PLAN
`prompts/04-baseline-capturer.md` — BASELINE
`prompts/05-task-executor.md` — EXECUTE
`prompts/06-merge-resolver.md` — RESOLVE
`prompts/07-build-verifier.md` — VERIFY (build + lint + unit + xml-validate)
`prompts/08-e2e-tester.md` — VERIFY (end-to-end / Playwright)
`prompts/09-code-reviewer.md` — REVIEW
`prompts/10-fix-applier.md` — FIX
`prompts/11-recapture-and-deliver.md` — RECAPTURE
`prompts/12-deployer.md` — DELIVER

section.reference: Skeleton each prompt file must follow.

section.title: Skipping phases
section.p: A playbook may declare phases that do not apply. Examples:

list.item: Knowledge methodology batch — skip BASELINE / RECAPTURE; VERIFY runs only `validate-methodology-xml.py`.
Backend ETL fix — skip BASELINE / RECAPTURE; VERIFY adds DAG smoke checks.
Storybook story addition — skip BASELINE (no "before" exists for new stories); RECAPTURE is the only visual proof.

section.p: The orchestrator still walks the phase list in order — it simply dispatches no subagent for a skipped phase and records the skip in its run log under `.aidocs/<project>/in-progress/<batch-id>/run-log.md`.
